title: Agentic Engineer
description:
**agentic engineer**


**job description: agentic engineer**


**company overview**



rotolo consultants, inc. (rci) is one of north america’s fastest\-growing landscaping companies. we are actively transforming how the green industry uses technology — moving beyond simple data collection to building sophisticated, ai\-powered workflows and custom software solutions. if you want to design and deploy autonomous agents that have a tangible impact on large\-scale field operations, join rci and grow the future with us.

  


**the role**



as an **agentic engineer**, you will own the design, development, and deployment of ai\-powered agentic workflows across rci’s technology stack. this is not a prompt engineering role — you will build real systems: autonomous pipelines, llm\-driven automation, tool\-calling agents, and integrations that replace manual processes at scale.



you will work directly with the director of technology strategy and operations and the software development team to identify where agentic workflows create the most business value — and then build them. you’ll bring both engineering rigor and product instinct: not just “can we automate this” but “should we, and what does the right system look like?”



this role is also expected to bring engineering maturity to how our development team operates — including lifecycle discipline, code quality standards, and planning processes the team is actively building.



this is an in\-person position based at rci’s corporate headquarters in slidell, la.

  


**key responsibilities**


* **agentic workflow design:** architect and build autonomous, llm\-driven workflows that reduce manual effort and improve operational efficiency across rci’s business.
* **tool\-calling \& integration:** design and implement tool\-calling agents using claude code, openrouter, mastra ai, and the vercel ai sdk. integrate agentic systems with internal platforms (cortex, rci service now, quickbase) and third\-party services.
* **pipeline development:** build and maintain automation pipelines triggered by business events — using github, webhooks, and event\-driven architectures to connect systems and eliminate manual steps.
* **ai strategy contribution:** serve as an active voice in identifying and prioritizing automation opportunities. this role is expected to help lead ai strategy at rci — not just execute it.
* **code quality \& lifecycle discipline:** bring engineering maturity to agentic systems: strict typing, test coverage, monitoring, and deployment standards. help establish and model best practices across the dev team.
* **stakeholder translation:** convert business processes and operational pain points into well\-scoped technical requirements. communicate clearly to non\-technical stakeholders about what agents can and cannot do.
* **evaluation \& iteration:** build evaluation frameworks to measure agent accuracy, reliability, and business impact. continuously improve based on real\-world performance data.

  


**technical stack**


* **ai / agentic:** claude code, mastra ai, openrouter, vercel ai sdk, model context protocol (mcp)
* **languages:** typescript / javascript (es6\+)
* **frontend:** js / react
* **backend / api:** js, trpc, rest, webhooks, event\-driven architecture
* **database:** postgresql (supabase), sql, prisma orm
* **infrastructure:** vercel, vps / nginx, github, ci/cd pipelines
* **monitoring:** sentry (error tracking and ux insights)
* **tools:** git, github actions, unit and end\-to\-end testing frameworks (jest / vitest / cypress)

  


**preferred skills \& experience**


* **agentic systems experience:** you have designed, built, and shipped autonomous or semi\-autonomous llm\-powered workflows — not just called the openai api.
* **product instinct:** you think about what to build, not just how. you ask whether a workflow should exist before you build it. you obsess over ux and reliability from the end user’s perspective.
* **full\-stack competence:** you can own a feature end\-to\-end — database schema, backend logic, api layer, and front\-end surface.
* **engineering rigor:** you write typed code, test it, monitor it in production, and document it. you raise the bar for the team around you.
* **business context orientation:** you understand that the agent serves the business, not the other way around. you measure your work in outcomes, not output.
* **ai tool fluency:** you use ai\-assisted development tools aggressively and intelligently — and you know how to verify the output.
* **quickbase knowledge:** (optional) experience or willingness to learn quickbase for legacy data integration and automation triggering.

  


**compensation \& benefits**


* **salary:** $80,000 annually
* **insurance:** medical, dental, vision, and supplemental options.
* **retirement:** 401k plan with partial match.
* **time off:** paid vacation, sick leave, and holidays.

  


**education \& requirements**


* **education:** bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or a related technical field — or equivalent demonstrated experience. bootcamp graduates with strong portfolios will be considered.
* **experience:** 2\+ years of professional software development experience, with at least one production agentic or ai\-integrated system you can speak to in depth.

title: AI Architect
description:
about us:

qisg leverages quanta’s comprehensive resources to deliver collaborative solutions for our partners' energy infrastructure needs. we use in\-house talent, expertise and resources to plan, design, engineer, manage, conduct maintenance on and construct projects.  

our turnkey service capabilities provide our customers with efficiency, consistency, attention to detail and safe execution. the qisg team brings together engineering, safety, quality, material procurement, qa/qc, right\-of\-way acquisition, scheduling, environmental planning, permitting, title and land management expertise that ensure outstanding results for our clients.
about this role:
**ai architect**  

**location:** in\-office, monday–friday, core hours 8:00 am–5:00 pm
katy office: 15011 katy freeway, houston, tx 77094  

qisg leverages quanta’s comprehensive resources to deliver collaborative solutions for energy infrastructure needs. we bring together engineering, safety, quality, procurement, qa/qc, scheduling, environmental, permitting, and land management expertise to design and execute complex infrastructure projects with efficiency, consistency, and safety at the core.  

we are now driving a major transformation in how work gets done through ai—embedding intelligent systems into business processes to accelerate execution, improve decision\-making, and scale expertise across the organization.
what you'll do:

as an ai architect, you will design, build, and deploy ai\-driven solutions that directly support business operations across qisg. you will work embedded with cross\-functional teams to solve high\-impact problems, rapidly prototype solutions, and turn proven ideas into scalable production systems.  

you will:* lead high\-impact ai engagements end\-to\-end, owning discovery, scoping, solution design, and delivery for complex, executive\-visible initiatives
* build rapid ai prototypes that demonstrate immediate business value and evolve into production\-ready systems
* productionize ai solutions, ensuring they are scalable, maintainable, secure, and usable in real operational environments
* develop agentic workflows and ai\-powered automation to streamline complex business processes across engineering, procurement, finance, legal, and operations
* enable knowledge extraction and intelligence from unstructured and structured data sources (documents, spreadsheets, systems, and images)
* automate and optimize manual workflows such as reporting consolidation, validation processes, document review, and operational tracking
* mentor and upskill team members and business partners, helping drive broader ai adoption and capability across the organization
* define and promote ai best practices, reusable patterns, and accelerators that improve speed and consistency across engagements


you will apply ai to real operational challenges such as:* building agentic workflows that streamline multi\-step business processes
* extracting structured insights from large volumes of technical documents and enterprise data
* automating manual workflows such as invoice validation, reporting reconciliation, and backlog reduction
* consolidating financial and operational reporting across multiple systems
* enabling searchable knowledge systems for engineering, legal, and project teams
* creating ai\-powered tools that unlock new ways to discover, analyze, and act on enterprise data


what you'll bring:
### **required**

* 8\+ years of experience in software engineering, ai/ml engineering, data engineering, solutions architecture, or technical consulting
* bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, information systems, mathematics, or related field (or equivalent experience)
* strong software engineering fundamentals including version control, testing, ci/cd, and production\-grade development practices
* proficiency in modern programming languages such as python and/or typescript/javascript
* strong sql skills and experience with relational databases and modern data platforms
* hands\-on experience building with modern ai systems including llms, rag, agents, prompt engineering, and tool use frameworks
* experience taking ai or ml systems from prototype to production, including monitoring, evaluation, governance, and scaling
* demonstrated ability to lead complex, multi\-stakeholder initiatives from problem definition through delivery and impact measurement
* experience with at least one ai coding or agent tool (e.g., claude code, cursor, copilot, codex)
* awareness of enterprise security, data governance, and responsible ai practices
* strong communication skills with the ability to engage executives, technical teams, and field stakeholders

### **preferred**

* experience in energy, utilities, epc, infrastructure, or construction environments
* experience working directly with non\-technical stakeholders in fast\-paced delivery settings
* familiarity with claude, anthropic apis, mcp servers, or similar ai ecosystems
* experience building reusable frameworks, accelerators, or internal platforms
* background in customer\-facing or embedded solution delivery roles


what you'll get:
* competitive base salary
* generous pto \& paid holidays to maintain work\-life balance
* 401(k) with a generous match to plan for your future
* comprehensive benefits – medical, dental, vision, and life insurance
* weekly pay for consistent, reliable compensation
* great mentoring \& career growth opportunities – we invest in your development

title: GCP Cloud Engineer - REMOTE in US
description:
**req id:** 372476
  
  

ntt data strives to hire exceptional, innovative and passionate individuals who want to grow with us. if you want to be part of an inclusive, adaptable, and forward\-thinking organization, apply now.
  
  

we are currently seeking a gcp cloud engineer \- remote in us to join our team in plano, texas (us\-tx), united states (us).
  
  

**prior to applying, please review and comply accordingly:**  

\*\* must be a us citizen or green card holder to proceed with applying.
  
  

\*\* please know, an fbi cjis background check is administered every 12 months.
  
  

\*\*this position aligns with supporting a 24\*7 operations production support model.
  
  

**key responsibilities:**  

cloud operations support
  
* provision, configure, and maintain cloud infrastructure across oci and aws, azure, gcp .
* monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve incidents, performance issues, and service outages in production and staging environments.
* implement and maintain monitoring, alerting, and logging solutions to ensure high availability and reliability.
* lead root cause analysis and post\-mortem documentation for major incidents.
* execute patch management, upgrades, and regular maintenance activities.
* develop and maintain backup, disaster recovery, and failover strategies and operations.
* participate in on\-call rotation and after\-hours support as required.
* automation infrastructure management
* develop and maintain infrastructure as code (iac) templates using tools such as terraform, cloudformation, arm, or oci resource manager.
* use scripting (e.g., python, bash, powershell) to automate repetitive tasks and operational processes.
* champion the use of configuration management tools and assist in devops pipeline integrations.
* recommend and implement cost optimization, resource utilization, and rightsizing strategies.


security compliance
  
* ensure adherence to security best practices, including least\-privilege access, encryption, and network segmentation.
* implement and manage identity and access management (iam) policies and roles.
* monitor, identify, and remediate security vulnerabilities reported by scanning tools or external advisories.
* support compliance efforts related to customer and regulatory requirements (txramp, iso, soc2, etc.).


collaboration documentation
  
* work closely with application, security, and network teams for solution delivery and support.
* mentor junior engineers and provide technical guidance as needed.
* create and update technical documentation, runbooks, and sops.
* participate in client calls to provide technical input when required.


**requirements:**  

* 3\+ years of hands\-on experience in cloud engineering, operations, and/or support (provisioning, configuring, securing, supporting, and optimizing cloud\-native and hybrid workloads in aws, azure, gcp, and/or oci)
* 3\+ years multi\-cloud experience (must have hands\-on in at least 2 of aws/azure/gcp/oci; familiarity in all is preferred.)
* experience in scripting languages (python, bash, powershell, etc.).
* experience with ci/cd pipelines, containerization (docker, kubernetes), and automation tools (terraform, cloudformation, arm templates, etc.).


where required by law, ntt data provides a reasonable range of compensation for specific roles. the starting pay range for this role will depend on the nature of the role offered and will either be $70,000 \- $105,000 . actual compensation will depend on a number of factors, including the candidate’s relevant experience, technical skills, and other qualifications.
  
  

this position may also be eligible for incentive compensation based on individual and/or company performance. if the position offered in temporary, the position will not be eligible for incentive compensation.
  
  

this position is eligible for company benefits that will depend on the nature of the role offered. company benefits may include medical, dental, and vision insurance, flexible spending or health savings account, life and add insurance, short and long term disability coverage, paid time off, employee assistance, participation in a 401k program with company match, and additional voluntary or legally\-required benefits.

ntt data is a $30 billion business and technology services leader, serving 75% of the fortune global 100\. we are committed to accelerating client success and positively impacting society through responsible innovation. we are one of the world's leading ai and digital infrastructure providers, with unmatched capabilities in enterprise\-scale ai, cloud, security, connectivity, data centers and application services. our consulting and industry solutions help organizations and society move confidently and sustainably into the digital future. as a global top employer, we have experts in more than 50 countries. we also offer clients access to a robust ecosystem of innovation centers as well as established and start\-up partners. ntt data is a part of ntt group, which invests over $3 billion each year in rd.
  
  

whenever possible, we hire locally to ntt data offices or client sites. this ensures we can provide timely and effective support tailored to each client’s needs. while many positions offer remote or hybrid work options, these arrangements are subject to change based on client requirements. for employees near an ntt data office or client site, in\-office attendance may be required for meetings or events, depending on business needs. at ntt data, we are committed to staying flexible and meeting the evolving needs of both our clients and employees. ntt data recruiters will never ask for payment or banking information and will only use @nttdata.com and @talent.nttdataservices.com email addresses. if you are requested to provide payment or disclose banking information, please submit a contact us form, https://us.nttdata.com/en/contact\-us.
